title: Worker ask-and-wait + autonomous-loop escalation for lieutenant comms
description:
Implements open questions 2 & 3 from docs/specs/worker-lieutenant-comms.md.

Today worker->lieutenant comms (shipped in PR #268) is fire-and-forget notify only. A worker that hits a fork ('which approach do you want?') cannot ask and wait for an answer, and in the autonomous pull loop there is no human pane to ask.

Scope:
- Let a worker post a question and block until the lieutenant replies (watch its own pane or a reply file), distinct from fire-and-forget notify.
- Autonomous pull-loop fallback: when no notification_pane is configured, route the worker's question through orca's existing stuck/escalation path so it surfaces as an escalation instead of being lost.
- Keep the stateless-daemon contract: no per-task state in memory.

This is the key gap for trustworthy unattended (pull-loop) beads delegation.

title: Phase 4: Complete-on-merge -> auto-unblock
description: finishAssignment -> Complete(Merged)=bd close --suggest-next; Release on escalate/cancel (lease model C).

title: Phase 1: worksource package + interface + ManualSource
description: internal/worksource: WorkItem, Outcome, ErrAlreadyClaimed, Source interface, no-op ManualSource. Zero behavior change. TDD red->green.

title: Phase 2: BeadsSource (shells bd --json) + Verify
description: Exec bd --json; ErrAlreadyClaimed via 'already claimed' string on non-zero exit; parse stdout only (Linear nag on stderr); Verify bd identity (version --json + dolt subcommand). Fixtures in internal/worksource/testdata.

title: Phase 3: idle-pull dispatch loop (opt-in, default OFF)
description: monitorTickPull in runLoop; free clones -> Ready -> Claim -> existing assign path; filter epic-type out of ready; fail-fast Verify at startup.

title: Adopt beads as a worksource in orca
description: Pull-based dependency-aware dispatch via a worksource.Source interface + BeadsSource. orca stays a thin executor; Linear stays system of record. Plan + status: Linear LAB-1922.

title: orca mail: swap assign send-keys convention -> orca mail
description: Replace the notifyConvention body in internal/daemon/assign_prompt.go with the `orca mail send` form, keeping the same notify-pane resolution + omit-when-unresolved behavior (assign.go:91 / resolveNotifyPane assign.go:306). Inject worker identity (issue-derived sender, resolved recipient, thread=issue). Fleet branch: remote-host workers keep send-keys; local workers get orca mail. Update/repoint tests asserting the old amux send-keys convention text.

title: orca mail: docs + verify + ship
description: Docs + verify + ship. Update orca-design.md "Notifications" to reference orca mail; mark worker-lieutenant-comms.md open-question #1 resolved. Run targeted new/changed tests with -count=100 (repo flake rule), go vet ./..., full make test, coverage >=80% patch. Review pass + simplification pass. Open PR with Motivation/Summary/Testing/Review-focus.

title: orca mail: SQLite mail store + schema
description:
Add a `mail` table + store methods to the SQLite backend (internal/daemonstate/sqlite.go), mirroring the existing `events` table.

Columns (portable types only): id (AUTOINCREMENT PK), project, thread_id, sender, recipient, body, importance (low|normal|high|urgent), kind (note|ask|reply), ask_id (nullable), reply_body, read_at (nullable TEXT ts via formatTime), created_at. Indexes: (project,id), (project,recipient,id), (project,thread_id,id).

Methods: SendMessage, Inbox(unread), MarkRead, Reply. Define the mailbox store interface + Message type in a new severable internal/mailbox package (no import of the daemon FSM). TDD: extend store_contract_test.go so the same suite runs against this backend. Reference: events DDL sqlite.go:114, AppendEvent sqlite.go:1918.

title: orca mail: Postgres mail store + schema
description:
Mirror the `mail` table + store methods in the Postgres backend (internal/daemonstate/postgres*.go). Same schema as the SQLite child but with the three backend differences: BIGSERIAL PK, TIMESTAMPTZ timestamps, $N placeholders. Reference: events DDL postgres.go:106, AppendEvent postgres_write.go:539, Events stream postgres_query.go:450.

Confirm cross-backend parity by running the shared store_contract_test.go suite against Postgres. Emit new event kinds mail.sent / mail.replied via AppendEvent.

title: orca mail: CLI verbs send/ask/inbox/read/reply (+--watch)
description: Wire `orca mail send|ask|inbox|read|reply` in internal/cli. `--watch` on inbox built on the existing Events(project, afterID) stream filtered to mail.* (reuse orca events plumbing). `ask --wait` = worker-side poll loop (no daemon goroutine); non-zero exit on timeout so the worker can proceed on a default. Help-registry entries; `orca mail` no-verb help stub. Table-driven tests + golden output for inbox formatting.

title: orca mail: structured worker↔lead messaging channel
description:
Replace raw `amux send-keys` as the worker→lead channel with **`orca mail`**: a structured, background message store in orca's own state backend. The worker writes (`orca mail send` / `ask`); the lead pulls (`orca mail inbox [--watch]` / `read` / `reply`). Nothing is injected into the lead's PTY.

Design + Decision Log: docs/specs/worker-lead-messaging.md

Key decisions (locked with Charles):
- **Build, not adopt `am`** (MCP Agent Mail). Rejected for: non-permissive "MIT + OpenAI/Anthropic Rider" license; single-host SQLite+git arch vs orca's multi-host single-coordinator store; 72MB pre-1.0 single-maintainer nightly-Rust runtime dep. Kept its message schema as a design reference.
- **In orca**, as a severable `internal/mailbox` package (no import of the task FSM; extractable later). Not a standalone repo.
- **Name:** `orca mail <verb>` (send|ask|inbox|read|reply).
- **Semantics:** notify (fire-and-forget) + ask-and-wait (worker-side poll loop; no daemon goroutine — preserves stateless-daemon contract).
- **Identity:** orca auto-registers each worker on assign; sender = issue-derived, thread_id = issue id.
- **Storage:** new `mail` table written for BOTH SQLite and Postgres (mirrors the existing `events` table; portable column types only — no JSONB/arrays).
- **Consumption:** `orca mail inbox --watch` rides the existing `Events(project, afterID)` stream filtered to `mail.*`. New event kinds `mail.sent` / `mail.replied`.
- **Scope:** local-first. Fleet/remote workers keep `send-keys` until a later shared-store step.

Supersedes the old "orca notify (event log + post to pane)" framing of this issue. Children track the implementation phases (see task_plan.md). orca-y5r.9 (ask-and-wait) depends on the CLI verbs child.

title: Stop benign worker.merge_notify_failed / task.completion_failed on successful merge
description:
Observed on LAB-1965 (#271) and LAB-1969 (#274): every successful codex merge emits worker.merge_notify_failed and task.completion_failed AFTER the task already reached done. Root cause: the post-merge notify/completion step runs 'amux wait idle' on the worker pane, but the postmortem command orca just sent keeps the pane busy, so the idle-wait times out and logs a failure for a task that actually completed cleanly.

Fix: do not gate task completion on pane idle, and/or do not emit a *_failed event once the task is already terminal (done). Makes the delegated-task lifecycle stop looking like it failed when it succeeded.

title: Fix: worksource pull loop poison-retry of failed dispatches
description: Bug B from pull-loop validation. A failed dispatch must not re-dispatch the same item every tick. See LAB-1945.

title: Fix: assign Linear status write-back non-fatal for non-Linear IDs
description: Bug A from pull-loop validation. Make assign's Linear SetIssueStatus non-fatal when the id is absent from Linear (beads-native ids); worker must still spawn. See LAB-1944.

title: Phase 6: docs + config + fleet bd install
description: Update orca-design.md, CLAUDE.md, config.toml; document bd (not br) requirement + clones-over-worktrees rationale.

title: Quiet issue.status_skipped for beads-only items with no linked Linear issue
description:
From the LAB-1965 secondary observation. A beads item with no external_ref Linear link emits issue.status_skipped on every status transition ('lookup issue <bead-id>: Entity not found'). The skip itself is correct (there is no Linear issue to update), but it is logged as if something went wrong on every transition.

Fix: detect beads-only items (no linked Linear id) and downgrade/suppress the skip to debug-level or omit it, so the event log isn't noisy for beads-tracked projects. Behavior unchanged; logging only.

title: Spec: worker spawner-awareness + notify/ask channel
description: Design spec for worker->lieutenant comms (alphazero pattern). See LAB-1946 + docs/specs/worker-lieutenant-comms.md.

title: worksource pull tick: emit per-tick observability trace
description: Add observability to the worksource pull tick. In internal/daemon/pull.go, a healthy tick (runPullTick / pullReadyWork) currently logs only on error, so idle and active ticks are invisible. Emit ONE concise log line per tick via the daemon's logf (match the style of the other daemon tick log lines) summarizing: free clone count, number of ready items, number dispatched, and number skipped due to worksource.ErrAlreadyClaimed. Do NOT log when the loop is disabled (workSourceEnabled false). Add a table-driven unit test with t.Parallel. Keep files under 500 lines; patch coverage at least 80 percent. Branch off origin/main. Open a PR titled with this issue id. Iterate until CI is green.

title: PM-1 (post-MVP): reflection nudge before retry
description: Optional profile nudge template; never auto-kills.

title: PM-2 (post-MVP): auto-rebalance before escalate
description: Release claim -> pull loop re-dispatches to free worker; design double-work race first; never auto-kills.

title: Phase 5 (deferred): delete internal/linear; beads owns Linear
description: Collapse issueTracker into worksource; enable bd linear --push/cron. Sign-off-gated (trades real-time Linear status for cron-lag).
